overview.technical: A minimal test fixture that exercises every required field in ProjectAnalysis with the smallest possible content.
overview.layman: A tiny example project used to make sure the report system works.

tech_stack.technical: No dependencies; this fixture exists solely for smoke-testing.
tech_stack.layman: There are no real tools used by this fixture — it is just a test placeholder.

architecture.technical: Single-file architecture. No layers, no modules.
architecture.layman: Imagine a single sticky note. That is the entire project.

methodologies.technical: Smoke testing: assert the launcher produces well-formed HTML.
methodologies.layman: We check that the report builder is not broken before we trust it.

pattern_rationale.technical: Minimal fixture chosen to verify schema compliance with the smallest valid payload.
pattern_rationale.layman: We use a tiny example so we can quickly check things work.

pitfalls.technical: No real pitfalls in a fixture.

entries.title: This is a test fixture, not a real project
entries.technical: Treat any insights as non-actionable; the fixture is synthetic.
entries.layman: This is just a test, so do not learn anything serious from it.
